stories.title: Schwarber's Record Chase — 18th HR, 6 Straight, Mattingly Next
stories.angle: Schwarber hit HR No. 18 on Thursday (417 feet, 2-run shot, 8th inning off Samaniego) as Phillies beat Red Sox 3-1. That's 6 consecutive games with an HR — 7 HRs in that span. He's 2 games from Don Mattingly's all-time MLB record of 8 straight (set 1987). The manager he's playing for: Don Mattingly. The irony is complete. Also: 18 HRs leads all of MLB. Phillies 12-4 under Mattingly.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber hit his 18th HR last night. 🔴⚾

6 consecutive games with a homer — MLB's all-time record is 8.

The man who set that record in 1987: Don Mattingly.

Schwarber's current manager: Don Mattingly.

Two games away from making history. 👀

#Phillies #MLB
x_posts.tweet_type: FOLLOW UP (from May 14 "going for 6th straight" coverage — today he confirmed it)

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Schwarber's current stretch: 7 HRs in 6 games.

He leads all of MLB with 18 on the season.

Phillies are 12-4 under Mattingly. From 9-19 to this.

Baseball is good. 🔴⚾

#Phillies #MLB
x_posts.tweet_type: FOLLOW UP (from May 14 "going for 6th straight" coverage — today he confirmed it)

stories.title: Ben Brown's Starter Breakthrough — Cubs Blank Braves 2-0 in Shutdown
stories.angle: Ben Brown was a reliever for 12 games to start 2026. His second start Thursday: 4 IP, 1 H, 7 K, 0 ER in 65 pitches. Eight combined scoreless innings across 2 starts. Ian Happ's 424-ft solo HR off Chop House completes the Cubs' 2-0 shutout. Cubs avoid Braves sweep, snap 4-game losing streak. Cubs now 28-16, Braves ~30-14.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Ben Brown started 2026 as a reliever. 12 appearances out of the pen.

He moved to the rotation and hasn't allowed a run in 8 innings across 2 starts.

Ian Happ hit one 424 feet off the Chop House.

Cubs 2, Braves 0. Four-game skid over. 🐻⚾

#Cubs #MLB

stories.title: The NL Picture in May — Braves vs Cubs vs Phillies
stories.angle: After Thursday: Braves ~30-14 (NL's best), Cubs 28-16, Phillies 22-22 (12-4 under Mattingly). The Braves have the best record in baseball. The Cubs won Thursday's finale to deny Atlanta the sweep and stay only 2 games back. The Phillies under Mattingly are now a genuine Wild Card threat. NL East vs NL Central hierarchy is forming in May.

stories.title: Tampa Bay's Remarkable May — 28-13 Despite Three Starters Gone
stories.angle: Tampa Bay leads the AL East at 28-13 with starting pitchers Pepiot (hip surgery, out for season), Matz (elbow IL), and Boyle (IL) all unavailable. The Rays have done it through opener strategy, walk-off wins, and depth across the roster. They're now the AL's most complete team despite not having a traditional ace.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The Tampa Bay Rays are 28-13 and leading the AL East.

Pepiot: out for season (hip surgery).
Matz: on the IL (elbow).
Boyle: on the IL.

Three starting pitchers gone and they're STILL winning. 🤯

This team is built different. ⚡

#RaysUp #MLB
x_posts.tweet_type: FOLLOW UP (new angle: 28-13 season total and depth story vs May 14's "16 wins in 18 games" angle)
